title: menowise signal
description: One claim from one source about one entry-subject. This is the info-archetype signal contract, FROZEN as kdc defined it (ADR-0045) and emitted verbatim by gn_info_scout — its fields are fixed; the menopause/midlife-health domain is expressed through the profile's attribute vocabulary (data/profiles/scout.md), NOT by changing this shape. Append-only; conflicting signals coexist by design (signal > canon). Provenance carries the literature citation: source_url = DOI/PMID/journal URL, publisher = the journal, source_date = publication year.

id.description: sig-YYYYMMDD-<slug>; must equal the filename stem.

site_id.description: Ref: data/sites/<id>.yml (the canonical entry). Null while the subject is an unmatched candidate.

site_hint.description: Free-text subject identification when site_id is null (the topic/symptom/intervention as reported). Omit the key entirely when site_id is set.

attribute.description: What the claim is about, from the profile's attribute vocabulary: study_type, sample_size, population, intervention, comparator, outcome, effect, safety, guideline_recommendation, prevalence, definition, …

value.description: The claim, raw, as reported by the source — no normalization here.

source_url.description: The citation URL, verbatim: a DOI (https://doi.org/…), PubMed (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/…), or the journal/guideline URL.

publisher.description: The journal or issuing body (e.g. 'JAMA', 'Cochrane Database Syst Rev', 'The Menopause Society').

source_date.description: Publication date, as precise as the source gives (year is common for literature).

collected_by.description: Gnome name + run id, or console identifier.

confidence.description: SOURCE-level trust for this single claim (primary literature / guideline body = high; secondary summary = medium; lay aggregator/headline = low). NOTE: this is provenance trust, NOT study-evidence strength — evidence_strength is resolved on the entry by gn_info_records from the clustered study_type/sample_size signals.
